channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Body of missing SpaceX employee found at Texas beach
item.description: The body was recovered by Cameron County local authorities at Boca Chica Beach near the original point where he was lost shortly after 6:30 p.m. on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Salinas, NewsNation

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump denigrates Harris as 'lazy,' invoking a racist trope against Black people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump on Tuesday called his Democratic opponent Kamala Harris “lazy," criticizing the vice president with a word long used to demean Black people in racist terms.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump and Harris seek Latino votes by touting their approach to the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump promoted economic policy Tuesday as their best chance to win Latino voters. But their approaches are very different.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cartel using 'bracelets' to track whether migrants paid to cross
item.description: The Texas Department for Public Safety took NewsNation to a river where crews came across several discarded paper bracelets.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: Jurors watch video extracted from Libby German's phone
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. – One of the most famous—or infamous—pieces of the Delphi murders puzzle is video taken from Libby German’s phone. The grainy image of “Bridge Guy” was taken from a video extracted from Libby’s phone.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: US charges Iran Revolutionary Guard official in alleged plot to kill a human rights activist in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday charged a senior Iranian military official and three others with links to that country's government with plotting to kill an Iranian American author on U.S. soil — a development lauded by the alleged target. “This is a beautiful day for me.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted for murder in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School has pleaded not guilty.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in North Carolina on Election Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — North Carolina voters will cast ballots for president and governor in the Nov. 5 general election while many in the state are still dealing with the aftermath and massive destruction caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: A sheriff’s office statement and data from the state show that the North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Asheville annual holiday tradition canceled due to Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (WSPA) - An annual national holiday tradition that has taken place for over three decades in Asheville has been canceled due to the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio man charged with bringing massive 'Trump' sign to Capitol for rioters to use as a weapon
item.description: An Ohio man was arrested Tuesday on charges that he brought a massive “Trump” sign to the U.S. Capitol and joined other rioters in using it as a weapon against police officers during a mob attack.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photos: Rhode Island zoo welcomes baby two-toed sloth
item.description: "Fiona is an excellent momma, and they are bonding beautifully," the zoo wrote in a social media post Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Doiron, NewsNation

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, WGN 9

item.title: Meeting scheduled to talk safety concerns after Ohio library brawl
item.description: The goal of the meeting is not to determine a solution but to understand perspectives after a brawl between teenagers at the Dayton Metro Library.
item.dc:creator: Adrienne Oglesby, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Trump favorability ratings among lowest for nominees since 1956: Gallup
item.description: Former President Dwight D. Eisenhower received the best favorability rating in Gallup’s 1956 poll, with 84 percent supporting him.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: How third-party candidates could make a difference in swing states
item.description: Third-party candidates will be on the ballot in all of the top battleground states, drawing increased scrutiny over how they could influence the outcome of the presidential election in two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrants caught at border bussed, flown out of San Diego in possible 'cover up' before election: official
item.description: A San Diego County official criticized the flying of illegal immigrants out of California to other states so close to the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Former Iron Maiden singer Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name Paul Di'Anno, has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Autopsy finds 'pink cocaine' in Liam Payne's system
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: One of the last remaining WWII Navajo Code Talkers dies at 107
item.description: One of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II using the tribe's native language has died, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Giant Pumpkin Regatta makes a splash
item.description: Paddlers dressed in their Halloween best raced in carved-out pumpkins with all their might as part of the West Coast Giant Pumpkin Regatta.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, Fox 5

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Ohio officer stands trial in the 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer has gone on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crash in Houston kills 4, topples radio tower, officials say
item.description: The private helicopter with four people on board, including a child, either struck a cable or the tower, Houston Police Chief J. Noe Diaz said at a news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From showgirl feathers to shimmering chandeliers, casino kitsch finds new life
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Crystal chandeliers that once glimmered above a swanky lounge, bright blue costume feathers that cloaked shimmying showgirls, and fake palm trees that evoked a desert oasis are just some the artifacts making their way from the latest latest casino graveyards of Las Vegas into Sin Ci
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why experts say keeping standard time is 'undeniably' better for us
item.description: Surveys have found that most Americans want to do away with the twice-a-year changing of the clocks. But whether we should observe daylight saving or standard time year round will depend on who you ask.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9
